description_long: This sculpture depicts a dynamic, lifelike owl in mid-flight, with its wings dramatically spread and feathers rendered in intricate detail. The surface has a rough, weathered texture, suggesting it is crafted from stone or bronze with a patinated finish. The wings are broad and slightly tattered at the edges, giving a sense of movement and age. The body is compact and muscular, with the head slightly lowered as if the owl is about to land or capture prey. The coloration is predominantly earthy browns and grays, enhancing the naturalistic appearance. The overall form is expressive and captures the essence of a bird of prey in motion.
description: A detailed, naturalistic sculpture of an owl in mid-flight with outstretched wings and a weathered, earthy surface.
description_view_0: The owl is seen from the front, with both wings fully extended and the head slightly lowered, emphasizing the bird's powerful chest and forward motion.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the curvature and span of the wings, with the body and tail partially obscured by the wing closest to the viewer.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back of the owl and the full extension of the wings, with feather details and the tail visible.
description_view_3: An angled view from behind and to the side, showing the layered feathers and the dynamic twist of the wings.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible in the images; the background is plain and unadorned.
